clinical_history: What combination of conduction disturbances is present?
diagnosis: The ECG shows sinus rhythm with complete right bundle branch block (RBBB) with left anterior fascicular block (LAFB).  The former causes the classic RSR-prime; morphology in V1; the latter causes the left axis deviation.   Note: the subtle variation in P wave morphology here is due to baseline artifact and not to wandering atrial pacemaker. (Possible left atrial abnormality is present--see V1 on magnified view.) Also note that the calculation of the frontal plane QRS axis with RBBB is most rigorously based on the first 80-100 ms of the widest QRS. The minuscule r waves in III and aVF are not diagnostic, but raise consideration of a prior inferior MI, in concert with the bifascicular block. An echocardiogram to assess ventricular size and function might be useful if indicated.
